task_completion.description: Open the starting rocket design and access the fin set editor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: OpenRocket application is open with 'aft_heavy_rocket.ork' loaded. The fin set configuration dialog/editor is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Increase the fin sweep length until the stability margin is >= 1.2 calibers.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Sweep length value in the fin set editor is increased. The stability margin indicator (typically in the top right corner of the main window or above the rocket diagram) shows a value of 1.2 calibers or greater.

task_completion.description: Run the existing flight simulation to verify flight dynamics.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Flight simulations' tab is open in OpenRocket, and a simulation run shows as completed (e.g., a green checkmark, flight data populated, or a plot window is open).

task_completion.description: Save the modified rocket design to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog or file manager shows the file 'stable_swept_rocket.ork' saved in the '/home/ga/Documents/rockets/' directory, or the OpenRocket window title reflects the new filename.

task_completion.description: Write an engineering memo detailing the sweep changes, final stability, and an explanation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor is open showing 'sweep_optimization_memo.txt' in '/home/ga/Documents/exports/'. The text contains the initial sweep length, the final sweep length, the final stability margin, and a brief explanation of why increasing sweep improves stability.

integrity.description: Verify that Fin Height, Root Chord, and Fin Position were not altered.
integrity.visual_evidence: In the fin set editor screenshots, the Root Chord and Fin Height values should not appear modified from their baseline, and the fin set's position relative to the bottom of the rocket must remain unchanged.

integrity.description: Verify that no mass components were added and the GPS tracker was not removed.
integrity.visual_evidence: The component tree in the OpenRocket main window does not show any new mass components (e.g., no 'Mass component' added to the nose cone) and the 'GPS Tracker' is still visible in the aft section of the rocket.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the OpenRocket GUI to perform the modifications and simulations.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show actual interaction with the OpenRocket graphical interface (clicking tabs, editing fields, running simulations) rather than just editing the .ork XML file in a text editor or using terminal commands.
